master_row.title: Mode Quantization in Confined Geometries
master_row.canonical_statement: Quantum modes become discrete when fields are confined; their allowed frequencies depend on the region's size, shape and curvature.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating n, m.
master_row.dictionary: For fields confined to a bounded region ( M), allowed modes satisfy boundary conditions determined by the Trawinistic manifold (Dirichlet, Neumann, or mixed) and have discrete frequencies (_n) determined by the eigenvalues of the Trawinistic Laplacian on ().
master_row.encyclopedia: Geometry and boundary conditions discretize allowed field frequencies. Interpretive role: This entry is the spectral bridge from curved fields to finite-geometry effects. It prepares the way for boundary operators and Casimir-type energies by showing exactly where the discrete mode list comes from.
